steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Command of Evidence (Textual) question asking why Miguel accompanies his brother. The answer must be grounded directly in what the passage states.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Miguel has not received formal instruction in poetry and wants to ask the sisters' famous father to be his mentor.': The passage never says Miguel lacks instruction or that he wants a mentor. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Miguel has learned all his brother can teach him and now desires to be taught by the sisters.': It is the narrator, not Miguel, who has learned all her tutor can teach. The passage says nothing about Miguel wanting instruction from the sisters. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Miguel hopes to recite his own poetry to the sisters and solicit their comments on his work.': The passage never mentions Miguel wanting to recite or share his work. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Miguel anticipates having the opportunity to be introduced to both the sisters and their father.': The passage states directly that Miguel "was hoping not only to meet us but to make the acquaintance of the poet himself," which captures both goals — meeting the sisters and meeting their poet father. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
